meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no portfolio. Learn to implement strategic SaaS pricing using Antigravity and Claude Code to land high-paying clients fast.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends when you stop building for yourself and start building for high-value business outcomes. By deploying a professional SaaS pricing page using Antigravity and Claude Code, you showcase technical architecture and commercial awareness, proving to clients that you don't just write code—you engineer revenue-generating assets.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio page is the graveyard of a thousand freelance careers. You have the skills, but without a concrete artifact to show a client, you remain a commodity, fighting for scraps on low-bid platforms. The tension is palpable: you need the job to get the experience, but you need the experience to get the job.

Relief arrives through strategic engineering. Instead of crafting 'to-do' lists, you will build a high-conversion SaaS pricing module. This isn't just a UI exercise; it is a signal to your clients that you understand the mechanics of growth. By leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code, you will transform from a novice with no history into an expert who ships production-ready SaaS infrastructure.

steps.title: Define the Revenue Model
steps.description: Establish a three-tier pricing structure (Basic, Pro, Enterprise) to demonstrate your grasp of SaaS business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React-based pricing table component with three tiers: Starter, Pro, and Scale. Include toggleable monthly/annual billing states with a 20% discount highlight.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a responsive PricingTable component with Tailwind CSS and Framer Motion animations.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Schema
steps.description: Structure your pricing data to be dynamic, allowing for easy updates without hardcoding values.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing plan including features list, price, and CTA text.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the hardcoded pricing data into a constants.ts file with a robust TypeScript interface.'

steps.title: Implement Conversion Optimization
steps.description: Add psychological triggers like 'Most Popular' badges to guide user decision-making.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a 'Most Popular' badge to the Pro tier with a subtle pulse animation and a border-highlight effect.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add a highlighted border and a badge to the Pro pricing card to increase visual hierarchy.'

steps.title: Integrate Interactive Billing
steps.description: Ensure the UI handles state changes for billing cycles seamlessly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a state management hook to toggle between monthly and annual pricing, recalculating costs dynamically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement a billing cycle toggle that updates all prices in the PricingTable component via React state.'

steps.title: Deploy and Showcase
steps.description: Push your component to a live environment to create a shareable link for potential clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment-ready configuration for the pricing component using Vercel settings.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Prepare the project for deployment and generate a README that explains the business value of this pricing architecture.'

pro_tips: Always include a 'Contact Sales' button for the Enterprise tier to signal you understand high-ticket B2B workflows.
Use micro-animations on hover to make the pricing table feel premium; clients equate smooth UI motion with high-end development skill.
Store pricing data in a JSON file separate from the component logic so you can easily show clients how you build maintainable codebases.
Add a 'Frequently Asked Questions' section directly below your pricing table to address common sales objections—this shows you think like a marketer.
Use descriptive, professional variable names (e.g., 'annualBillingMultiplier' instead of 'x') to make your code look like a senior engineer wrote it.
resources: Tailwind CSS Pricing Component Patterns: https://tailwindui.com/components
content.next_steps: Once your pricing component is live, link it on your LinkedIn and Twitter profiles as a 'High-Conversion SaaS Module' case study to attract your first paying client.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn a blank portfolio into a revenue-focused showcase that clients can't ignore. Stop waiting for permission to be an expert and start shipping the code that builds businesses. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
